name: Mejorado

migrationPath.notes: Found growing primarily in Saraguro region; widely assumed to be Typica before genetic testing

description: Mejorado is widely known by the colloquial name 'Typica Mejorado,' but genetic testing by World Coffee Research revealed it is actually a cross between Bourbon and an Ethiopian Landrace variety — not Typica at all. This is a clear example of how local names can diverge significantly from genetic reality. The variety is grown primarily in Ecuador's Saraguro region and produces a distinctive, bright, floral cup. Its name ('mejorado' = 'improved' in Spanish) likely refers to observed improvements in yield or cup quality over nearby Typica plants.
